qualifiedClaim.summary: ARC is comptroller-capable software: it ships a cryptographically signed, fail-closed economic control plane with explicit operator-facing control surfaces, partner-visible receipt and settlement contracts, and bounded federated multi-operator proof on qualified surfaces.
constraints: The proof remains bounded to locally qualified operator, partner, and federated evidence packages.
This gate proves comptroller-capable architecture and bounded proof packaging, not ecosystem-wide market dominance.
The broader market-position claim still requires external adoption and dependence thresholds.

nonQualifiedClaims: universal partner dependence beyond the documented qualified surfaces

externalThresholds.summary: Multiple independent operators run ARC as a live economic control surface.
Partners consume ARC evidence as authoritative inputs for billing, settlement, dispute, audit, or underwriting workflows.
Meaningful economic workflows would break or lose partner acceptance without ARC.

gateConditions.summary: Economic control surfaces are externally operable and explicitly documented.
Receipt, checkpoint, reconciliation, and settlement artifacts are packaged as partner-visible contract surfaces.
Bounded multi-operator federation proof is explicitly packaged and reproducible.
The authoritative docs separate comptroller-capable proof from the stronger market-position claim.
